DM_COUNTDATA

The DM_COUNTDATA structure is used (indirectly) by the DmRegisterPerformanceCounter function.

typedef struct _DM_COUNTDATA {
    LARGE_INTEGER CountValue;
    LARGE_INTEGER RateValue;
    DWORD CountType;
} DM_COUNTDATA, *PDM_COUNTDATA;

Members

CountValue
Depends on CountType. See below.
RateValue
Depends on CountType. See below.
CountType
Some combination of the DMCOUNT_* constants. See below.

CountType

Every counter, when queried, needs to have the same base type (Event, Counter, or PRatio) in its CountType as the counter is registered with. Beyond that, the type data can be different, and is used in a way that varies from counter to counter. The value returned in CountType reflects the actual data presented in the count data.

Value Counters

DMCOUNT_VALUE—gets the current value of the counter, in CountValue. RateValue is unused.

DMCOUNT_VALUE | DMCOUNT_AVERAGE—gets the moving average of the counter, over the number of 100-millisecond intervals (from 100 ms to 1 sec) specified in the low four bits (DMCOUNT_FREQUENCY) of the requested type. The average value is CountValue / RateValue.

Event Counters

DMCOUNT_EVENT—gets the current value of the counter, in CountValue. RateValue is unused.

DMCOUNT_EVENT | DMCOUNT_PER*—gets the event data as a time-based rate. The data is evaluated over a number of 100-millisecond intervals (as above). The returned value (CountValue / RateValue) is the ratio of the change in the event counter to the change in time. Time is measured either in frames (DMCOUNT_PERFRAME), seconds (DMCOUNT_PERSEC), milliseconds (DMCOUNT_PERMSEC), or system ticks (DMCOUNT_PERTICK). A system tick is approximately equal to one millisecond, but may not equal exactly one millisecond. Note that only the ratio (CountValue / RateValue) can be used to interpret the value; neither component is guaranteed to represent either the difference in the event counter or the amount of time passed.

Ratio Counters

DMCOUNT_PRATIO—gets the ratio data over time. The data is evaluated over a number of 100-millisecond intervals (as above) as the ratio of the progressive difference of two values; essentially, the ratio of two progressive rate counters. The value is returned as the ratio (CountValue / RateValue). The data is the ratio of the difference between the current and previous numerator value to the difference between the current and previous denominator value.

Requirements

Header: Declared in XbDm.h and XboxDbg.h
Import Library: Use XbDm.lib for target system code; and XboxDbg.lib for development system code.
